title: Sore o Nasumono (Translation)
wikitext:
Translations for the contents of Yamashita & Kio's book [[Sore o Nasumono]]. =Neon Genesis Evangelion TV Last Scenario Prot= This is the original text and the English translation of the unused TV Series ending scenario, translation by [[User:RockManBalls|RockManBalls]]. Corrections by Xard. This transcript is currently incomplete. ==English Translation== It might be surprising given what ultimately resulted, but during the initial development of the Anime series, Supervisor Anno took on something of a passive role, and staff would regularly bounce ideas off of him in order to flesh out the story. As time passed, and I was put in charge, I received an early scenario manuscript by fax. When I went to return the fax, I jotted down some left over ideas just off the top of my head. I still get a good laugh out of it to this day. ==Original Japanese== 作品の内容からは意外な印象かもしれませんが、庵野監督はアニメ制作の初期段階においては受け身の姿勢をとり、客スタッフにアイデアを出させぶつけ合うことで、話の骨格に肉付けをします。てな経過から、設定担当である私のところにも脚本の初期稿がファックスでやってきます。それについて思いついたことは電話で返すのですが、ファックスで返却するとこのように内容が残り、あとから見かえすとなかなか笑えます。 =Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film= This is the original text and the English translation of the unused Movie scenario, translation by [[User:RockManBalls|RockManBalls]]. This transcript is currently incomplete. ==English Translation== ''Page74''
'''Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film Novel'''
"The Long-Awaited People."
[Translator's Notes: "Gekijouban shousetsu," meaning the book based on the movie that was never made.] During the development of the film, Yamashita, being asked to submit ideas, thought of an original story he'd conceived mid-way through the television show. Created in collaboration with Kio [omitted]*, the utterly ridiculous work was eventually presented to Supervisor Anno. Rather than a sequel to Evangelion TV series, contents of the script are better described as All Star Show featuring the whole cast. [*Translator's Notes: As I know it, "氏宅" is a placeholder for someone who doesn't want to give their name, usually their surname. Like "Takashi S氏宅." ] The last Angel had been defeated. Having just confronted the Angel threat, humanity began to wield EVA against EVA. As this abuse of EVA technology continued, an enormous conflict erupted. This is the era in which our story begins. ''Page 75''
During this conflict, NERV General Headquarters' Special Forces Division, along with EVA 01, attacked NERV's Euro-Germany Branch. Having confirmed the existence of "that," EVA 01 expanded its AT-Field, and NERV completely destroyed the facility with a neutron bomb. The "that" they were looking for was the Sinking-Type Equipment Universal Invasion Gun (Field Sinker), code-named the "Spine of the Death God." This new type of corrosive AT-Field worked by ways of a guaranteed-to-hit heavy particle bullet, which, when used against enemies with current AT-Field capacity, would almost always find its target with terrible speed. At the same time as construction of this secret weapon was underway, the Euro-Germany Branch used their political will to secure the return of their creation, EVA 02. In a single move, NERV Germany hid EVA 02 and the "Spine of the Death God" in accordance with their master plan. Before their troublesome dealings could be discovered by the international community, they were ultimately stopped by the NERV organization proper. Although NERV's Euro-Germany Branch has since been dismantled, as well as their brilliant achievement, EVA 02, this incident would serve as a catalyst, forever linking EVA technology to global military strategy. However, this turmoil would serve only as a small part of a greater disaster. How will these mysteries come to light?
Will they gather the technological strength they need?
Now, all is but a mystery...
However, those humanoid mechs have been built.
Humanity's creations.
But those creations are EVA. EVA has proven to be distinctly different, continually defying current technology. From the very beginnings of civilization, a powerful mental contamination was underway. Humanity had always been set in a certain direction...... ''Page 76''
[Translator's Notes: Handwritten text in bottom-right reads: Thick beams of light shoot out from here. The light should be fine and particled, gradually getting brighter in target area. Despite radiating light in all directions, it should look like it's firing inwards.] A contamination not unlike brainwashing gradually drove into each and every person. At first it infected the staff involved in the creation of the humanoid mechs. Soon followed the organization, the city, and finally the entire country. All began to move with a single purpose: they were commanded to build an EVA in their own image. Fortune. Does this name refer to it's nature, or that of it's existence? It is unknown what nation constructed Fortune. For the nation responsible was itself burnt to the ground... And it was from that day on that a worldwide mental contamination began. Beasts in the shape of men run throughout a ruined city. They do not speak. They do not use tools. They run on all fours. They communicate only with powerful gazes in order to protect their territory and the prosperity of their brood. They are wolf-men. When this mental contamination first infected normal humans, they lived what could be described as a life of culture. But eventually this mental contamination became an organic one, and in a short period of time, their bodies transformed into something like the werewolves of fairy-tales. And this abnormality is not confined to a single city. Now, throughout the world, this destructive form is common among human beings. Boasting this world-wide influence, the composition of humanity has changed drastically. Human beings, as intellectual life-forms, is left with only a whisper of the remaining populous, with the amount of destruction only accelerating. Those still remaining live in constant fear of this previously unseen, and unusual, contamination. Within a ruined city, standing erect atop the rubble of the city's countless buildings, the wolf-men look to the sky. Together they face the moon and howl. The view from the monitor displayed a structure in the shape of a pyramid. Flames surround the outlying ruins of this perfect geometric structure, glistening horribly as it suppresses the invading wolf-men. This pyramid is NERV's Central Experimental City. It is no exaggeration to say that it has truly become humanity's final fortress. In this city, they wield meager Jamming Technology to bear the onslaught. Namely, they've been able to disrupt the mental contamination waves, while maintaining the facility's structural integrity, through use of Interference Wave Oscillation. Because of this, those within the walls are protected from the mental contamination waves pouring down on nearly the rest of the entire world. The technology itself was originally used in EVA's experimental phase of construction, used to protect against mental disaster (Psychological Hazard). In a word, the use of this technology has spared these lucky people from total mental contamination. In the center of the facility, in a bored out hole mirroring the shape of a mortar, EVA 01 stands ready. An Electrical Phase Contact Unit resembling feathers or a tail has been installed onto EVA 01's back, allowing her to operate on her own volition, without needing a pilot. This Reflector Hole, as it's now called, was once used as an orbital observation system receiver, but has since been converted into an EVA "private room." As if listening out for something, EVA 01 watches the starry sky above. In a roof-top corridor outside the facility, Hyuga and Aoba looked to EVA curiously. "She looks like some kind of a poet or philosopher, doesn't she?" Aoba said. "She certainly looks like she's thinking about something," agreed Hyuga. "When EVA goes off to battle, before the pilot unleashes her rage, EVA 01 seems so calm and intellectual... Like she's trying to compose an understanding of something she can't completely grasp, but still knowing more than just a small notion. Who knows what she's thinking." ''Page 77''
"I don't know either..." Aoba said, lowering his gaze as he looked outside the facility. Beyond the line of fire, in the center of a rubble-covered road, Aoba caught a glimpse of the distant shadows of the wolf-men. "But I think those guys outside think about even less. Still, don't they seem to be getting a little more... sophisticated? Or... something like that. I actually think it's somewhat incredible." Aoba turned to Hyuga. "I know that must sound weird, huh?" "You're not alone. People are just afraid to say it," Hyuga said, looking, too, at the wolf-men gathered on the road. "They suddenly attack the very thing we're supposed to protect. In truth, there are only a few people in this entire facility. This equipment prevents us from going outside, not to mention we only have our emergency stores to live on... Still, we've managed to keep on fighting one way or another, even though we're up against an enemy we still don't fully understand." He took a deep breath. "The people of this city try to act normal, but they're troubled. They can't help but depend on wishful thinking. They're looking through EVA's eyes to the future." Unknown if she was truly thinking or not, EVA continued to stand alone in deep meditation. Every engineer in the facility had piled into the Plug Regulation Room, investigating something in 01's Entry Plug. Using a fiber scope--something resembling a gastrocamera--they looked over an exposed circuit board. Two engineers in the plug's interior enlarged the camera's view with a computer display. "Hold on!" Engineer A yelled. "Go back a little! Hey, wait, what is that...?" "There's no doubt about it!" Engineer B said. He turned to address the others. "Quickly! Bring Dr. Akagi over here!" They saw through the Neuro-Computer Zoom Matrix that a single large synapse had wrapped itself around a nerve cord. "There must be a leak on the side of the Basic Order Line's cut-feedback," Ritsuko announced. "Is it true that EVA did this itself? What is the possibility that it was re-written by a third party?" "There's no evidence of anti-body program or structural defense failure," Engineer A said. "Of course, in theory, it is possible to trick the anti-body program all together. Still, irregular fluctuations have been recorded. We can only assume that EVA's own consciousness might have re-written the information. The irregularity was recorded roughly one month ago... That would put it during EVA 01's Deep Stage accident..." Ritsuko, without using the fiber scope, had crawled into the narrow cylinder and over the seat of the plug. Pointing a small lighted loupe, she quickly located the problem. She lightly furrowed her brow just as the engineer finished speaking. He continued: "I can't explain why, but that incident seems to be the cause of this." "There seems to be no question about it..." Ritsuko said. Climbing her way out of the plug, Ritsuko lost her grip and began to slide down. Without a moments delay, an engineer grabbed her hand and pulled her back up. "Thank you..." she said. "We need to know what kinds of effects this circuit-change will have on the pilot during operation." She dropped down to the main floor and looked over her team of engineers. "Are there any suggestions?" They all wore grave expressions. Engineer C said flatly, "There shouldn't be any effect." ---- '''In a previous battle''' A nude image of shinji yelled out boldly, letting loose a tremendous punch. Moving in concert, EVA doubled his actions. Shinji had become the EVA itself. This is what is called the Deep Stage. The punch struck a tank-like robot, but its shield and EVA's AT-Field collided, nulling any contact. "01 to Control!" Shinji shouted. "This guy's harder than I thought!" Shinji's voice echoed inside the Entry Plug, but he hadn't spoken aloud. A visor over his eyes flickered strange patterns of light, while his body radiated a soft, glowing light as he laid back in the plug's seat as if in a deep sleep. "I'm authorizing use of the Monoatomic Ribbon," Misato ordered. "What about back-up from 00?!" Shinji said. "Is Ayanami in battle position yet?!" ---- Engineer C's voice was getting smaller and smaller as he spoke. "Normal synchronization or not, using Super Synchronize during Deep Stage will cause the pilot's consciousness to be completely transferred to EVA," he said. "The pilot will begin to think that he, himself, is EVA." "However," Engineer A said, "while his consciousness is absent, his body will cease to function. Until the pilot's mind returns, EVA will be in control of his body." "In that scenario," Engineer B said, "EVA's brain is no longer being controlled by the host computer, nor is Shinji in control of his body. Put simply, EVA and the pilot will have exchanged both their minds and bodies. Operating with the Basic Order Line cut, and without receiving commands from the body control program, we believe EVA's consciousness will flood rapidly into the pilot's body." "No, that's not possible," Engineer D said. "Recordings from prior experiments have shown that the pilot will most certainly die from ego collapse. The structure of EVA's mind is completely different from a human's. If EVA's mind enters Shinji and he is unable to control it, he will cease to be!" Asuka stood on the gate outside the facility. Those inside the fortress didn't know she was there. In this world where EVA and their pilots are increasingly in a position of great importance, Asuka was nonetheless labeled a traitor, despite being unaware of the political strategizing of NERV Germany. ''Page 78''
Relieved to be free of suspicious eyes, standing alone outside while wearing Heavy Interference Equipment was better than being thought a criminal. Watching the dizzying flames, she noticed the shadow of a person emerge from an underground gate. But wait. He wasn't wearing any interference equipment. An unexpectedly bold-faced Shinji, completely unaware that Asuka was behind him, walked to the raised bridge behind the barricade of fire. Walking faster and faster, he was eventually sprinting at such an accelerated rate that it was hard to make him out. What Asuka saw next was even more startling. Shinji kicked off violently from the bridge's foundation, leaping through the air and beyond the wall of fire, disappearing in an instant. '''The city of rubble''' Asuka had lost sight of Shinji. Moments later, Asuka's mind was racing with fear and regret, completely obsessed with Shinji's unusual behavior. And not to mention he had set foot in the wolf-men's city under moon-light. NERV had weapons, but going out there alone was madness. Fearful of the wolf-men's howls, Asuka nonetheless searched for Shinji with her eyes. Then, a wolf-men child cut in front of her view and Asuka followed it as well. The child, dragging along it's clothing and lacking any of Asuka's fear, began to chase after Shinji. After spotting something fly out from a narrow passage and leap to the very top of a building, Asuka thought she had stopped breathing. There glowing in the moonlight was Shinji. From where she was standing, Asuka saw the countless wolf-men turn to him and bow. ''Page 79''
Together they began howling to him, smiling as Shinji ran over to them playfully. Rather than being afraid, Asuka was taken aback by the almost bizarre beauty of the scene. Suddenly, Shinji shot a calm and beckoning gaze to her as tears fell from his eyes. That gentle expression seemed to come not from the person in front of her, but from someone else entirely. Their everlasting bond seemed gone... Inside the NERV facility, walking out of the cage leading to the Reflector Hole, Ritsuko, leading the team of engineers, disregarded the protests from the maintenance personnel. "Please wait! 01 is only at Level B cool-down!" As the gate to the Hole opened, Ritsuko turned to EVA 01. "Shinji-kun! Is he inside EVA right now?!" The maintenance personnel were shocked. Everyone looked to 01. EVA gave no indication. And the wolf-men, too, gazed to the silent moon. They had gathered atop the ruined building, and each looked to the moon and whined. Illuminated under the moonlight, Shinji stood in the center of the wolf-men. Asuka reluctantly began to run away from where she was. Shinji quickly understood that her pride wouldn't allow her see anymore. He wore an expression both bashful and puzzled, yet somewhat hopeful. He lifted his arms and pulled in the air as if to embrace her fleeing body. Asuka fled, as if in a surreal dream. ---- "I can't do that, Ritsuko-san!" Shinji yelled. "Wasn't that the mental correction before?!" In the medical room designed for the pilots, Shinji resisted the medical scanner. Aside from the medical staff, agents from the Security Agency were also present. "If the latest memories are interrupted, it may go wild!" "Shinji-kun!" Ritsuko yelled. Shinji froze, but it wasn't from Ritsuko's cutting voice. The security personnel had drawn their pistols, and were pointing them right at him. ----- Shinji ran into Ayanami in the hallway. "Oh, Ayanami," he said. Once she laid her eyes on Shinji, her body began to tremble and she looked terrified. When he touched her shoulder, she shrugged off his hand and ran quickly down the corridor. Something's changing..., Shinji thought to himself. ----- It was a cloudy day. Misato was spearheading the External Area Investigation Department in an operation employing Unit 00 with Unit 01 functioning in an auxiliary role. The Investigation Members were collecting blood from a severed wolf-man arm. "This district's no good," Investigation Member A said. "When people become wolf-men, they forget how to prepare food and they'll eat anything they can get their hands on," Investigation Member B said. "They're prone to exposure from pollution and any hazardous materials out here." "That settles it," said Misato. "Shinji-kun, Rei, burn everything beyond the river." "Roger," Rei answered quickly. "Wh-wait a second, Misato-san!" Shinji said. "Those people aren't enemies, they're still human!" "I feel bad about it, too," said Misato. "But, Shinji-kun, we have to control this. If we leave it how it is now, other districts will be contaminated and more people will die." "But--" "I understand, Shinji-kun. To play God like this... I'm sure I'll go to hell for it. But we have to protect the people, no matter the cost... I hate it... If we don't succeed, we're only leading them to their doom." Shinji's trembling hand was rattling the control stick. The wolf-men watched EVA. As if with torch in hand, EVA burnt everything to the ground, like a fearful God. What are we protecting?, thought Shinji. The wolf-men were trying to escape as flames purged the ruined city. Is this something I have to protect? Will an answer come from protecting all of this? As the flames spread terribly over the earth, Shinji in the middle of it all, saw the clothes-wearing wolf-man child become enveloped in fire! At that moment under the moonlight, the wolf-men, without showing fear or even noticing, vanished in an instant. ---- '''In Nerv's Experimental City, Central Command Operations Room''' A stereo display ran a virtual model simulation of the current mental contamination waves pouring over the world. “If the moon itself isn't the source of the oscillation waves, is the surface reflecting them to us?” Gendo said. “Yes,” Ibuki said. “Because the mental waves are beams, measuring the direction is very difficult. Until now, we were unaware of the possibility due to the moon's revolutions in orbit and the beam's angle of incidence. Because of the wave's oscillation from Earth, the moon has basically become a giant reflector. We've already narrowed down the location of the source of the oscillation.” “Then all that's left is for us to go there, isn't there?” said Gendo. ==Original Japanese== ''Page 74'' 「新世紀エヴァンゲリオン」劇場版オリジナル小説
"彼方の待ち人"
作・山下いくと 劇場版制作にあたり、例によってアイデア提出を求められた山下が、テレビ版中盤あたりに思いついたオリジナルの話。きお氏宅で彼とともに、あーだこーだムダな話してつくったものを、庵野監督に見せることにしました。テレビ版EVA後日談というよりは、オールスターキャストショー的な内容にしています。 最終使徒は撃退された。
EVAをはさんで使徒と対峙していた人類は、そのとき初めてEVAそのものと向き合うことになる。
やがて始まったのはEVAに関する技術の乱用、そしてその争奪戦であった。
これはそんな時代を起点にして始まる話。 ''Page 75'' その日、ネルフ総括本部特殊部隊はEVA01とともにユーロドイツネルフ支局を急襲、゛それ゛を確認、ATフィールド展開の後、中性子爆弾使用によって施設を蒸し焼きにする。
それは、沈下型領界侵攻銃（フィールドシンカー）、コード゛死神の背骨〟。
新型の浸食性ATフィールドによって、レールがひかれたごとく、絶対の命中を保証された重粒子の弾体は、従来のATフィールドの反発力によってすさまじい速度を与えられて空間を滑り降り、目標に到達する。
極秘裏にこれを建造、同時に政治的手腕をもって当支部によって造られたEVA02を取り戻し、EVAさえ一撃で葬れるこの゛死神の背骨゛とEVA02 の２つをこれからの新たな世界ゲームのカードに加えようというドイツネルフのもくろみは、その裏国家間の駆け引きが露見する前に、ネルフという、一団体の中でのゴタゴタとして処理されてしまう。
ここにEVA０２建造という輝かしい業績を誇るネルフ・ユーロドイツ支部は解体されるのだが、この事件をきっかけに、世界は、EVA技術の有無がその国にとって今後の世界戦略の優秀につながることを重い知らされたのである。
しかしこの騒ぎも、これから起こる大災害の前では小さな事件として処理されることとなる。 どうやって機密をもちだしたのだろう
いかにして技術力を集めたのだろう
いまとなってはずべてが謎......
だがその人型は造られた
造ろうとしたのは人間
しかし造らせたのはEVA 従来の技術で造られたにもかかわらず、そのEVAは明らかに違っていた。
培養開始直後からそれは、まわりを強力に精神汚染し始めた。それも、ある指向性をもたせて......。
ある種の洗脳のようなその汚染に、人々は ''Page 76''
[Translator's Notes: Handwritten text in bottom-right reads: ぶっとい光線が出る
光線は細くちりちりいう
輝度を増してゆく
ターゲットはー方方向かうの
光線照射れもかかわらず
爆縮を起こす] だんだん侵されていく。
やがてその人型にかかわるスタッフは、施設は、都市は、国家は、ただひとつの目的のために動きだす。
隷従する人々に自らの身体を造らせるEVA。
″フォウチュン〟その名は自らの誇示か、それとも自らの存在に近い意味をもつのか、いまとなってはどこの国がフォウチュンを造ったのか、わからないし意味もない。
なぜなら、自らを造らせたその国をフォウチユンは焼きはらってしまったから……。
そしてその日からである、世界規模の精神汚染が始まったのは。 荒廃した都市の中を、人の形をした獣が駆けている。
ことばを話さず、視線とその力のみでわずかにナワパリと種族繁栄のためにコミュニケートし、道具を使わず4本足で駆けている。
彼らは人狼(ヒトオオカミ)、精神汚染を受けるまではれっきとした人間で、我々が言うところの文化的な生活を送っていた人々のなれの果てだ。
精神的な汚染は、やがて器質的異常を引き起こして、短期間のうちに身体そのものが、まるでおとぎ話の狼人間(ワーウルフ)のように変化してしまっている。そしてこの異常はこの地区に限ったことではない。いまや地球上全体で、人間にのみ起こっている共通で、かつ破壊的な現状なのである。
地球上て最大の勢力を誇っているのは、あいかわらず人間という構図に変化はないが、知的生命体という意味での人類は、これから描く極めてわずかな人々を除いて事実上、絶滅したと考えるほうが早く、残った人々も、日々精神汚染の恐怖におびえ、まるで先の見えない、異常な精神下の生活を送っている。
自らより破壊した都市、ピルというピルの瓦礁のてっぺんに人狼たちが立っている。
一斉に月に向かって吠える人狼。 画面をパンしていくと、底辺が三角形のピラミッドをいくつも合わせたような建造物がある。この完全な幾何学形態の建物は、まわりの廃虚の中ではかえって異彩を放っており、炎をめぐらせて人狼の侵入を抑えている。
これが、ネルフ中央実験都市。こんどこそ掛値なし、言葉どおりのか″人類最後の砦〟となってしまった。この都市には、それを支えるささやかなジャミング技術がある。すなわち、建物全体の形状と精神汚染波を撹乱する干渉波発振。この2つによって、かろうじて地球全土に降りそそぐ精神汚染から施設内の人聞を守っているのだが、その技術自体はもともと施設内においてEVAの運用実験時の精神災害(サイコロジカル・ハザード)を想定してのものであり、早い話、ここに勤務していた運のいい人間だけが精神汚染を免れたわけである。
施設中央に、すり鉢状にえぐられた鏡張りの巨大なホールがあり、EVA01が立ちつくしている。
背中に取り付けられた羽か尻尾のような位相差発電ユニットにより、EVA01はパイロットも乗せずに自らの意志、でここにたたずんでおり、このリフレクターホールはかつては軌道上監視システムのレシーバーだったのが、いまではEVAのプライベートルームと化している。
何かを聞いているように星空を見上げているEVA01。
施設外壁上の屋上回廊から日向と青葉がEVAを見ている。
青葉「まるで詩人か哲学者ってところだな」
日向「何かを考えているつてのは本当らしいよ。EVAは戦闘時に、パイロットが彼女の怒りのイメージに触れるまでは、極めて穏やかで知性的なんだ......。もっとも、思考の組み立て方がまるで違うのか、概念に差がありすぎるのか、何を考えているのかさっぱりわからないけどね」
青葉「ただ同じわからないにしても(青葉、体の向きを変えて、施設の外に視線を落とす。炎のラインの向こうの瓦礫の街の中、チラリチラリ人狼の陰が見え ''Page 77'' る)、連中とはずいぶん違うと思うぜ。何かもっと、こう、途方もなく高度な……なんていうのかな、とにかく凄いことさ(日向のほうを見て)……なんて考えるのは変かな……」
日向「君だけじゃないよ。誰も口にしないだけでね。（人狼の街を見て）俺たちは守るべきものをいきなり奪われてしまったんだ。まともなのはこの施設のわずかな人間だけ。装備がなくちゃ外にも出られないし、第一ここの備蓄だっていつまでもつことやら……。でも我々は何かと戦っているという、この災厄を招いた敵の正体すらまだつかめないでいるのにだ。(一息おいて)この街の人聞は正常であるがゆえに病んでいるんだ。だからなんでもいいから希望的なことを探さずにはいられない。EVAの視線の先にみんな未来を見ているよ」
まわりの思いを知ってか知らずか、瞑想するように立ちつくすEVA。 プラグ調整室。01のエントリープラグの中に何人ものエンジニアが潜り込み、何かを調べている。
胃カメラのようなファイバースコープで、プラグ内インテリアから引き出した回路基板の上をなぞっていく。

シンジ「そんな……これ……ぼく？」
いつの間にか、背後にいる父、碇司令。ハッとなって振り返るシンジ。
ゲンドウ「そうだ、世界規模の精神汚染を受けたあのとき、お前はここにいなかった」
モニターの声「銃を！麻酔銃を早く！！」
ゲンドウ「すでに、お前は重度に汚染されている」
モニターの中からの銃声に、ビクッと身を震わせるシンジ。
ゲンドウ「お前が正常でいられるのはEVAの中にいるとき、その影響下にあるときだけだ」 施設内を歩くシンジ。銃を携行した保安部員が両側を固めているが、そんなことは問題ではないようだ。彼を悩ませているのは自分自身。
目の前をストレッチャーを押す集団が慌ただしく走り抜ける。
ストレッチャーの上で暴れているのはミサトだ。
同僚の所員「押さえろ！」
所員A「瓦燦のすき間に落ちた人狼の子を助けようとして、シールドの外に長くいすぎたんだ！」
看護士「安定時聞を日分もオーバーしてる……うまくいっても後遺症が出るかもしれん！」
シンジの頭の中に碇司令の声が響く。
ゲンドウ「EVAから離れたとき、お前は人でなくなる。」 ネルフ本局司令部のフロアをただならぬ表情で歩いてくるリツコ、そのまま管制室の扉をくぐり抜ける。リツコ「EVA01、00との通信は、復帰しましたか！？」
冬月「いやまだだ。広侍域の電磁嵐はまだ続いているよ。回復の見込みはない」
伊吹「朗報もあります。先ほど青葉さんが、まだ生きている民間の衛星を見つけて軌道変更成功しました！1EVAとレーザー回線で通話できそうです」
青葉「とはいうものの、使える軌道に乗せるまでに丸1日はかかりそうです」
冬月「それより……先はど君の名前で出されたEVA01、00に対する作戦中止命令の理由を聞こう」 一同リツコを注視する。
コンソールへ進んで、内分のラボのコンピュータとつなぐリツコ。
リツコ「EVAを生み出すプロセスを……何度かのシミュレーションで、フオウチュンの誕生を確認しました。我々はEVAに対する価値基準を根本的に変える必要に迫られそうです」
ディスプレーに映し出される、分裂を続けるEVAの疑似細胞核。ところが途中から勾厳に遺伝情報が書き変わっていく。やがてそれが細胞そのものの変化に表われた瞬間、レーザーで光圧処理される。
ゲンドウ「続けたまえ」
リツコ「3体のか水準に達した。EVAがこの世に存在すると、その次には必ずフオウチュンが生まれる。これがEVAという種の絶対法則のようです」
日向「でもEVAは、すでに何体も存在していたじゃないですか。なぜいまになって？」
ゲンドウ「EVAはそれ自体が進化する」
冬月「その中でもお眼鏡にかなったEVAはわずかというわけか。しかし、フオウチュンはともかくEVAを造ったのは人間だ。種としての行動といえるかな」
リツコ「EVAはなぜ人型なのやでしょう。文明をもつ生物が、同じ形のものを見つけたら……」
冬月「興味をもつな」
リツコ「さらに科学技術が進歩すれば」
冬月「まず、調べるために複製してみて……！？ばかな！……初めから、原始の時代から仕組まれていたことだというのか！？」
ゲンドウ「タネはひとつだった。我々がそのタネを増やし、育てた」
リツコ「そして、収穫のときは来たのです」 フオウチュンのテリトリーに侵入しつつあるEVA01。
不思議ときっぱりとした顔のシンジ。
シンジ「あ」
地面に落ちている〝死神の背骨〟を拾い上げるEV ''Page 83'' A01。
シンジ「アスカの落とし物か……」
人狼からEVAの指し示すその向こうへ……いまシンジは、ヒ卜という存在がその聞を初抽出うものだと感じ始めている。
月下の瓦礁の衡を進むEVA01。右肩に〝死神の背骨〟を装備している。やや、遠巻きにして、人狼たちが後を追う。
川を飛び越えるEVA01。うずくまるように着地するほどの大ジャンプ。着地の音響が消えた無音の街に、衝撃で述統的にふれあう〝死神の背骨〟の、鱗状アーマーの金属的な残響が尾を引〈。
やがて、まわりにいた人狼の姿が消えていることに気付くシンジ。踏み越えてはならないエリアに侵入したらしい（実は1匹だけ、EVA01の後を追い続けている人狼がいる。聞に光る目は人狼のそれだが、その姿はアスカだ。廃櫨から廃他地へ飛び移り、EVA01を見下ろしている)。
フオウチュンのオブジェが一望できる丘の上に立つEVA01。
シンジ「さて……と」 すでに神の領域ごときは侵して久しく
少年の背の羽は黒く変わって抜け落ちた
すべてに近い人々が人狼となったいま
ヒトという存在は
限りなく自分ひとりの意味に近づき
全体の中の1個とする自分自身の認識を
彼は脱ぎ捨てつつある
彼はことごとくを打ち払ってでも前へ進む道を選ぶ (以上、プロローグ終わり) [[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Translations]]
html:
Translations for the contents of Yamashita & Kio's book Sore o Nasumono.

Contents

  • 1 Neon Genesis Evangelion TV Last Scenario Prot
    • 1.1 English Translation
    • 1.2 Original Japanese
  • 2 Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film
    • 2.1 English Translation
    • 2.2 Original Japanese

Neon Genesis Evangelion TV Last Scenario Prot[edit]

This is the original text and the English translation of the unused TV Series ending scenario, translation by RockManBalls. Corrections by Xard. This transcript is currently incomplete.


English Translation[edit]

It might be surprising given what ultimately resulted, but during the initial development of the Anime series, Supervisor Anno took on something of a passive role, and staff would regularly bounce ideas off of him in order to flesh out the story. As time passed, and I was put in charge, I received an early scenario manuscript by fax. When I went to return the fax, I jotted down some left over ideas just off the top of my head. I still get a good laugh out of it to this day.


Original Japanese[edit]

作品の内容からは意外な印象かもしれませんが、庵野監督はアニメ制作の初期段階においては受け身の姿勢をとり、客スタッフにアイデアを出させぶつけ合うことで、話の骨格に肉付けをします。てな経過から、設定担当である私のところにも脚本の初期稿がファックスでやってきます。それについて思いついたことは電話で返すのですが、ファックスで返却するとこのように内容が残り、あとから見かえすとなかなか笑えます。

Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film[edit]

This is the original text and the English translation of the unused Movie scenario, translation by RockManBalls. This transcript is currently incomplete.


English Translation[edit]

Page74
Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film Novel
"The Long-Awaited People."
[Translator's Notes: "Gekijouban shousetsu," meaning the book based on the movie that was never made.]


During the development of the film, Yamashita, being asked to submit ideas, thought of an original story he'd conceived mid-way through the television show. Created in collaboration with Kio [omitted]*, the utterly ridiculous work was eventually presented to Supervisor Anno. Rather than a sequel to Evangelion TV series, contents of the script are better described as All Star Show featuring the whole cast. [*Translator's Notes: As I know it, "氏宅" is a placeholder for someone who doesn't want to give their name, usually their surname. Like "Takashi S氏宅." ]


The last Angel had been defeated. Having just confronted the Angel threat, humanity began to wield EVA against EVA. As this abuse of EVA technology continued, an enormous conflict erupted. This is the era in which our story begins.


Page 75
During this conflict, NERV General Headquarters' Special Forces Division, along with EVA 01, attacked NERV's Euro-Germany Branch. Having confirmed the existence of "that," EVA 01 expanded its AT-Field, and NERV completely destroyed the facility with a neutron bomb. The "that" they were looking for was the Sinking-Type Equipment Universal Invasion Gun (Field Sinker), code-named the "Spine of the Death God." This new type of corrosive AT-Field worked by ways of a guaranteed-to-hit heavy particle bullet, which, when used against enemies with current AT-Field capacity, would almost always find its target with terrible speed. At the same time as construction of this secret weapon was underway, the Euro-Germany Branch used their political will to secure the return of their creation, EVA 02. In a single move, NERV Germany hid EVA 02 and the "Spine of the Death God" in accordance with their master plan. Before their troublesome dealings could be discovered by the international community, they were ultimately stopped by the NERV organization proper. Although NERV's Euro-Germany Branch has since been dismantled, as well as their brilliant achievement, EVA 02, this incident would serve as a catalyst, forever linking EVA technology to global military strategy. However, this turmoil would serve only as a small part of a greater disaster.


How will these mysteries come to light?
Will they gather the technological strength they need?
Now, all is but a mystery...
However, those humanoid mechs have been built.
Humanity's creations.
But those creations are EVA.


EVA has proven to be distinctly different, continually defying current technology. From the very beginnings of civilization, a powerful mental contamination was underway. Humanity had always been set in a certain direction......


Page 76
[Translator's Notes: Handwritten text in bottom-right reads: Thick beams of light shoot out from here. The light should be fine and particled, gradually getting brighter in target area. Despite radiating light in all directions, it should look like it's firing inwards.]


A contamination not unlike brainwashing gradually drove into each and every person. At first it infected the staff involved in the creation of the humanoid mechs. Soon followed the organization, the city, and finally the entire country. All began to move with a single purpose: they were commanded to build an EVA in their own image.


Fortune.


Does this name refer to it's nature, or that of it's existence? It is unknown what nation constructed Fortune. For the nation responsible was itself burnt to the ground... And it was from that day on that a worldwide mental contamination began.


Beasts in the shape of men run throughout a ruined city. They do not speak. They do not use tools. They run on all fours. They communicate only with powerful gazes in order to protect their territory and the prosperity of their brood. They are wolf-men. When this mental contamination first infected normal humans, they lived what could be described as a life of culture. But eventually this mental contamination became an organic one, and in a short period of time, their bodies transformed into something like the werewolves of fairy-tales. And this abnormality is not confined to a single city. Now, throughout the world, this destructive form is common among human beings. Boasting this world-wide influence, the composition of humanity has changed drastically. Human beings, as intellectual life-forms, is left with only a whisper of the remaining populous, with the amount of destruction only accelerating. Those still remaining live in constant fear of this previously unseen, and unusual, contamination. Within a ruined city, standing erect atop the rubble of the city's countless buildings, the wolf-men look to the sky. Together they face the moon and howl.


The view from the monitor displayed a structure in the shape of a pyramid. Flames surround the outlying ruins of this perfect geometric structure, glistening horribly as it suppresses the invading wolf-men. This pyramid is NERV's Central Experimental City. It is no exaggeration to say that it has truly become humanity's final fortress. In this city, they wield meager Jamming Technology to bear the onslaught. Namely, they've been able to disrupt the mental contamination waves, while maintaining the facility's structural integrity, through use of Interference Wave Oscillation. Because of this, those within the walls are protected from the mental contamination waves pouring down on nearly the rest of the entire world. The technology itself was originally used in EVA's experimental phase of construction, used to protect against mental disaster (Psychological Hazard). In a word, the use of this technology has spared these lucky people from total mental contamination. In the center of the facility, in a bored out hole mirroring the shape of a mortar, EVA 01 stands ready. An Electrical Phase Contact Unit resembling feathers or a tail has been installed onto EVA 01's back, allowing her to operate on her own volition, without needing a pilot. This Reflector Hole, as it's now called, was once used as an orbital observation system receiver, but has since been converted into an EVA "private room." As if listening out for something, EVA 01 watches the starry sky above.


In a roof-top corridor outside the facility, Hyuga and Aoba looked to EVA curiously. "She looks like some kind of a poet or philosopher, doesn't she?" Aoba said.


"She certainly looks like she's thinking about something," agreed Hyuga. "When EVA goes off to battle, before the pilot unleashes her rage, EVA 01 seems so calm and intellectual... Like she's trying to compose an understanding of something she can't completely grasp, but still knowing more than just a small notion. Who knows what she's thinking."


Page 77
"I don't know either..." Aoba said, lowering his gaze as he looked outside the facility. Beyond the line of fire, in the center of a rubble-covered road, Aoba caught a glimpse of the distant shadows of the wolf-men. "But I think those guys outside think about even less. Still, don't they seem to be getting a little more... sophisticated? Or... something like that. I actually think it's somewhat incredible." Aoba turned to Hyuga. "I know that must sound weird, huh?"


"You're not alone. People are just afraid to say it," Hyuga said, looking, too, at the wolf-men gathered on the road. "They suddenly attack the very thing we're supposed to protect. In truth, there are only a few people in this entire facility. This equipment prevents us from going outside, not to mention we only have our emergency stores to live on... Still, we've managed to keep on fighting one way or another, even though we're up against an enemy we still don't fully understand." He took a deep breath. "The people of this city try to act normal, but they're troubled. They can't help but depend on wishful thinking. They're looking through EVA's eyes to the future." Unknown if she was truly thinking or not, EVA continued to stand alone in deep meditation.


Every engineer in the facility had piled into the Plug Regulation Room, investigating something in 01's Entry Plug. Using a fiber scope--something resembling a gastrocamera--they looked over an exposed circuit board. Two engineers in the plug's interior enlarged the camera's view with a computer display.


"Hold on!" Engineer A yelled. "Go back a little! Hey, wait, what is that...?"


"There's no doubt about it!" Engineer B said. He turned to address the others. "Quickly! Bring Dr. Akagi over here!"


They saw through the Neuro-Computer Zoom Matrix that a single large synapse had wrapped itself around a nerve cord.


"There must be a leak on the side of the Basic Order Line's cut-feedback," Ritsuko announced. "Is it true that EVA did this itself? What is the possibility that it was re-written by a third party?"


"There's no evidence of anti-body program or structural defense failure," Engineer A said. "Of course, in theory, it is possible to trick the anti-body program all together. Still, irregular fluctuations have been recorded. We can only assume that EVA's own consciousness might have re-written the information. The irregularity was recorded roughly one month ago... That would put it during EVA 01's Deep Stage accident..."


Ritsuko, without using the fiber scope, had crawled into the narrow cylinder and over the seat of the plug. Pointing a small lighted loupe, she quickly located the problem. She lightly furrowed her brow just as the engineer finished speaking.


He continued: "I can't explain why, but that incident seems to be the cause of this."


"There seems to be no question about it..." Ritsuko said. Climbing her way out of the plug, Ritsuko lost her grip and began to slide down. Without a moments delay, an engineer grabbed her hand and pulled her back up. "Thank you..." she said. "We need to know what kinds of effects this circuit-change will have on the pilot during operation." She dropped down to the main floor and looked over her team of engineers. "Are there any suggestions?"


They all wore grave expressions. Engineer C said flatly, "There shouldn't be any effect."



In a previous battle


A nude image of shinji yelled out boldly, letting loose a tremendous punch. Moving in concert, EVA doubled his actions. Shinji had become the EVA itself. This is what is called the Deep Stage. The punch struck a tank-like robot, but its shield and EVA's AT-Field collided, nulling any contact.


"01 to Control!" Shinji shouted. "This guy's harder than I thought!" Shinji's voice echoed inside the Entry Plug, but he hadn't spoken aloud. A visor over his eyes flickered strange patterns of light, while his body radiated a soft, glowing light as he laid back in the plug's seat as if in a deep sleep.


"I'm authorizing use of the Monoatomic Ribbon," Misato ordered.


"What about back-up from 00?!" Shinji said. "Is Ayanami in battle position yet?!"



Engineer C's voice was getting smaller and smaller as he spoke. "Normal synchronization or not, using Super Synchronize during Deep Stage will cause the pilot's consciousness to be completely transferred to EVA," he said. "The pilot will begin to think that he, himself, is EVA."


"However," Engineer A said, "while his consciousness is absent, his body will cease to function. Until the pilot's mind returns, EVA will be in control of his body."


"In that scenario," Engineer B said, "EVA's brain is no longer being controlled by the host computer, nor is Shinji in control of his body. Put simply, EVA and the pilot will have exchanged both their minds and bodies. Operating with the Basic Order Line cut, and without receiving commands from the body control program, we believe EVA's consciousness will flood rapidly into the pilot's body."


"No, that's not possible," Engineer D said. "Recordings from prior experiments have shown that the pilot will most certainly die from ego collapse. The structure of EVA's mind is completely different from a human's. If EVA's mind enters Shinji and he is unable to control it, he will cease to be!"


Asuka stood on the gate outside the facility. Those inside the fortress didn't know she was there. In this world where EVA and their pilots are increasingly in a position of great importance, Asuka was nonetheless labeled a traitor, despite being unaware of the political strategizing of NERV Germany.


Page 78
Relieved to be free of suspicious eyes, standing alone outside while wearing Heavy Interference Equipment was better than being thought a criminal. Watching the dizzying flames, she noticed the shadow of a person emerge from an underground gate.

But wait. He wasn't wearing any interference equipment.

An unexpectedly bold-faced Shinji, completely unaware that Asuka was behind him, walked to the raised bridge behind the barricade of fire. Walking faster and faster, he was eventually sprinting at such an accelerated rate that it was hard to make him out. What Asuka saw next was even more startling. Shinji kicked off violently from the bridge's foundation, leaping through the air and beyond the wall of fire, disappearing in an instant.


The city of rubble


Asuka had lost sight of Shinji. Moments later, Asuka's mind was racing with fear and regret, completely obsessed with Shinji's unusual behavior. And not to mention he had set foot in the wolf-men's city under moon-light. NERV had weapons, but going out there alone was madness. Fearful of the wolf-men's howls, Asuka nonetheless searched for Shinji with her eyes. Then, a wolf-men child cut in front of her view and Asuka followed it as well. The child, dragging along it's clothing and lacking any of Asuka's fear, began to chase after Shinji. After spotting something fly out from a narrow passage and leap to the very top of a building, Asuka thought she had stopped breathing. There glowing in the moonlight was Shinji. From where she was standing, Asuka saw the countless wolf-men turn to him and bow.


Page 79
Together they began howling to him, smiling as Shinji ran over to them playfully. Rather than being afraid, Asuka was taken aback by the almost bizarre beauty of the scene. Suddenly, Shinji shot a calm and beckoning gaze to her as tears fell from his eyes. That gentle expression seemed to come not from the person in front of her, but from someone else entirely. Their everlasting bond seemed gone...


Inside the NERV facility, walking out of the cage leading to the Reflector Hole, Ritsuko, leading the team of engineers, disregarded the protests from the maintenance personnel.


"Please wait! 01 is only at Level B cool-down!"


As the gate to the Hole opened, Ritsuko turned to EVA 01. "Shinji-kun! Is he inside EVA right now?!"


The maintenance personnel were shocked. Everyone looked to 01. EVA gave no indication. And the wolf-men, too, gazed to the silent moon.


They had gathered atop the ruined building, and each looked to the moon and whined. Illuminated under the moonlight, Shinji stood in the center of the wolf-men. Asuka reluctantly began to run away from where she was. Shinji quickly understood that her pride wouldn't allow her see anymore. He wore an expression both bashful and puzzled, yet somewhat hopeful. He lifted his arms and pulled in the air as if to embrace her fleeing body. Asuka fled, as if in a surreal dream.



"I can't do that, Ritsuko-san!" Shinji yelled. "Wasn't that the mental correction before?!" In the medical room designed for the pilots, Shinji resisted the medical scanner. Aside from the medical staff, agents from the Security Agency were also present. "If the latest memories are interrupted, it may go wild!"


"Shinji-kun!" Ritsuko yelled.


Shinji froze, but it wasn't from Ritsuko's cutting voice. The security personnel had drawn their pistols, and were pointing them right at him.



Shinji ran into Ayanami in the hallway.


"Oh, Ayanami," he said.


Once she laid her eyes on Shinji, her body began to tremble and she looked terrified. When he touched her shoulder, she shrugged off his hand and ran quickly down the corridor.


Something's changing..., Shinji thought to himself.



It was a cloudy day. Misato was spearheading the External Area Investigation Department in an operation employing Unit 00 with Unit 01 functioning in an auxiliary role. The Investigation Members were collecting blood from a severed wolf-man arm.


"This district's no good," Investigation Member A said.


"When people become wolf-men, they forget how to prepare food and they'll eat anything they can get their hands on," Investigation Member B said. "They're prone to exposure from pollution and any hazardous materials out here."


"That settles it," said Misato. "Shinji-kun, Rei, burn everything beyond the river."


"Roger," Rei answered quickly.


"Wh-wait a second, Misato-san!" Shinji said. "Those people aren't enemies, they're still human!"


"I feel bad about it, too," said Misato. "But, Shinji-kun, we have to control this. If we leave it how it is now, other districts will be contaminated and more people will die."


"But--"


"I understand, Shinji-kun. To play God like this... I'm sure I'll go to hell for it. But we have to protect the people, no matter the cost... I hate it... If we don't succeed, we're only leading them to their doom."


Shinji's trembling hand was rattling the control stick. The wolf-men watched EVA. As if with torch in hand, EVA burnt everything to the ground, like a fearful God.


What are we protecting?, thought Shinji. The wolf-men were trying to escape as flames purged the ruined city.


Is this something I have to protect? Will an answer come from protecting all of this?


As the flames spread terribly over the earth, Shinji in the middle of it all, saw the clothes-wearing wolf-man child become enveloped in fire! At that moment under the moonlight, the wolf-men, without showing fear or even noticing, vanished in an instant.



In Nerv's Experimental City, Central Command Operations Room


A stereo display ran a virtual model simulation of the current mental contamination waves pouring over the world.


“If the moon itself isn't the source of the oscillation waves, is the surface reflecting them to us?” Gendo said.


“Yes,” Ibuki said. “Because the mental waves are beams, measuring the direction is very difficult. Until now, we were unaware of the possibility due to the moon's revolutions in orbit and the beam's angle of incidence. Because of the wave's oscillation from Earth, the moon has basically become a giant reflector. We've already narrowed down the location of the source of the oscillation.”


“Then all that's left is for us to go there, isn't there?” said Gendo.

Original Japanese[edit]

Page 74

「新世紀エヴァンゲリオン」劇場版オリジナル小説
"彼方の待ち人"
作・山下いくと


劇場版制作にあたり、例によってアイデア提出を求められた山下が、テレビ版中盤あたりに思いついたオリジナルの話。きお氏宅で彼とともに、あーだこーだムダな話してつくったものを、庵野監督に見せることにしました。テレビ版EVA後日談というよりは、オールスターキャストショー的な内容にしています。


最終使徒は撃退された。
EVAをはさんで使徒と対峙していた人類は、そのとき初めてEVAそのものと向き合うことになる。
やがて始まったのはEVAに関する技術の乱用、そしてその争奪戦であった。
これはそんな時代を起点にして始まる話。


Page 75

その日、ネルフ総括本部特殊部隊はEVA01とともにユーロドイツネルフ支局を急襲、゛それ゛を確認、ATフィールド展開の後、中性子爆弾使用によって施設を蒸し焼きにする。
それは、沈下型領界侵攻銃（フィールドシンカー）、コード゛死神の背骨〟。
新型の浸食性ATフィールドによって、レールがひかれたごとく、絶対の命中を保証された重粒子の弾体は、従来のATフィールドの反発力によってすさまじい速度を与えられて空間を滑り降り、目標に到達する。
極秘裏にこれを建造、同時に政治的手腕をもって当支部によって造られたEVA02を取り戻し、EVAさえ一撃で葬れるこの゛死神の背骨゛とEVA02 の２つをこれからの新たな世界ゲームのカードに加えようというドイツネルフのもくろみは、その裏国家間の駆け引きが露見する前に、ネルフという、一団体の中でのゴタゴタとして処理されてしまう。
ここにEVA０２建造という輝かしい業績を誇るネルフ・ユーロドイツ支部は解体されるのだが、この事件をきっかけに、世界は、EVA技術の有無がその国にとって今後の世界戦略の優秀につながることを重い知らされたのである。
しかしこの騒ぎも、これから起こる大災害の前では小さな事件として処理されることとなる。


どうやって機密をもちだしたのだろう
いかにして技術力を集めたのだろう
いまとなってはずべてが謎......
だがその人型は造られた
造ろうとしたのは人間
しかし造らせたのはEVA


従来の技術で造られたにもかかわらず、そのEVAは明らかに違っていた。

すさまじい勢いて光線を放ってくるフオウチュン。
廃嘘の都市を、光線を避けてまわるEVA02。倒壊したピル等もあまり遮蔽物として役に立たず、なかなか攻撃に移れずにいる。
アスカ「〝死神の背骨〟の目を覚まさせといて！並行してスーパーシンクロナイザー始動、私がディープステージにまで潜ってシンクロ率を上げたらどんなにおっかないか、見てらっしゃいよ！！」


Page 81

コンピュータ「ディープステージモード、シフトスタート」
アスカ「私の体に悪きしないでよ」
コンピュータ「繰り返してください」
バイザーからの光の中でぐったりしてゆくアスカ。いま、アスカの精神は体から切り離されEVAのボディに滋依しつつある。やがてコンソールからアスカの声。
アスカ「冗談よ」


上アゴのような銃口のカパーの後方にある補助グリップをEVA02の左手が引き寄せると、01の雄叫びのような砲障をあげて〝死神の背骨〟の銃口は、まさに怪獣の口のごとく開いた。同時に銃身の両側に並んだ鱗状の装甲が一斉に聞いて、内部にEVAの背骨のような銃身が見えたと思ったのも束の問、まわりの空聞を歪め始める。
銃を構えてピルの陰から飛び出す02。
もらった！とアスカは思ったが、どうしたことかロックオンできない。
アスカ「！？ちょっと！！何ビビってるのよ！この体の重さすぎる！」
なんとかトリガーを引き絞るアスカ、複雑な位相光を残して飛び去る弾体、フオウチュンから大きくはずれて背後の山脈へ。
アスカ「まずい！引きずっていく質量が、大きすぎる！」
弾体を包んでいた位相空間が崩壊し、空間がそこへ向かって吸い込まれるように（山脈の一部もごっそりもってゆかれる）小さくなったと思った直後に、その点が大爆発。アスカたちがいる所を合めて、まぶしい光の中、まるで散弾が一面に降りそそいでいるような状態になる。
その爆発の中で、アスカはフオウチュンが初めて組んだ腕を解くのを見た。同時に背中に寝かせてあったパイロンが肩の上に起き上がる。
アスカ「あなた……EVAなの！？」
両手首をフオウチュンに掴まれてしまう02。フオウチュンの一尉には自分が腕を組んでいたときに、手を入れておく手型がある。
その手型に、摑まれた子を入れられてしまう02。新たな異常に気付くアスカ。
アスカ「コンピュータ！スーパーシンクロナイズ・カット！ディープステージから、通常モードに引き戻して！いますぐ！」
コンピュータ「通常ステージ復帰シークェンス・スタート。108、107……」
アスカ「お願い！いますぐ戻して！！そんな……！？吸い込まれるよ！情報も……力も……私も……！なにもかもが……いやあ！！誰か助けて！(だんだん雑音混じりで聞こえなくなっていく)」


ネルフ本局指令♂部会議室。やられる寸前まで送られ続けていた02の情報は、アスカの声が消え入るとすぐノイズが走り、途絶えてしまう。
ゲンドウ「問題なのは、この新たなEVAよりもむしろ、この後ろに写っているオブジェの解析だ。進んでいるのか？」
伊吹「MAGI2は…………こんなことってあるのかしら……。とんでもない推測を突きつけてきました。フオウチュンに、3体以上と推測されるEVAを使って何かを建造しようとする意志を見いだせる。後ろの物体内に、エネルギーの流動や偏向のためのルートが多数見られるものの、サイクルとして機能するには分断箇所が多すぎることを〝未完成〟の理由であるとし、その分断箇所に沿ってEVAのパーツと適合する質所が多数存在したためこの結果に達したようです。この場合、必要なEVAをその数と配置パターンから最低3機としています」


長距離偵察型に換装されつつある00。そのパック


Page 82

アップとして2機の「震電」級陸上軽巡がフル積載の状態で控えている。
その足元を複数のネルフ局員が駆けている。炎をめぐらせた堀を越えて人狼が施設内に紛れ込んだらしい。


人狼は、施設内の入り組んだ通路を逃げている途中で、ひとりでいる綾波と出くわす。
一瞬、跨路した人狼だったが、すぐさま綾波に襲いかかる。
意外な素早さで人狼に払いを掛ける綾波。振り向きざま、すでに拳銃を梶っており、人狼の足を撃ち抜く。もんどりうって倒れる、人狼の悲痛な叫び。
綾波「私はつくられたものだから、あなたのことばは響かない。私にわかることばで話しなさい」
一発撃つ綾波。
綾波「私はケダモノがきらい。ケダモノの棲むヒ卜も嫌い」
さらに引き金を絞る綾波。


モニターディスプレーのみが光る一室で、コンソールに子をついたシンジが驚傍の表情て自分の映像を見ている。
映像は施設内の監規カメラのもので、暴れるシンジを保安部員2人が組み伏せている。
そこへ研究員を2人ともなった綾波が通りかかったとき、ものすごい力で大人2人を振り解くシンジ。
声を発するも、それはことばでなく、シンジは綾波に襲いかかっていく。
止めに入る人聞をことごとくはじき飛ばすシンジ。
衣服を引き裂かれ、床に組み敷かれるにいたって、初めて恐怖の表情を見せる綾波。
そして叫び。
モニターを見つめるシンジ。あまりのことにその表情のまま凍りついたようになっている。
シンジ「そんな……これ……ぼく？」
いつの間にか、背後にいる父、碇司令。ハッとなって振り返るシンジ。
ゲンドウ「そうだ、世界規模の精神汚染を受けたあのとき、お前はここにいなかった」
モニターの声「銃を！麻酔銃を早く！！」
ゲンドウ「すでに、お前は重度に汚染されている」
モニターの中からの銃声に、ビクッと身を震わせるシンジ。
ゲンドウ「お前が正常でいられるのはEVAの中にいるとき、その影響下にあるときだけだ」


施設内を歩くシンジ。銃を携行した保安部員が両側を固めているが、そんなことは問題ではないようだ。彼を悩ませているのは自分自身。
目の前をストレッチャーを押す集団が慌ただしく走り抜ける。
ストレッチャーの上で暴れているのはミサトだ。
同僚の所員「押さえろ！」
所員A「瓦燦のすき間に落ちた人狼の子を助けようとして、シールドの外に長くいすぎたんだ！」
看護士「安定時聞を日分もオーバーしてる……うまくいっても後遺症が出るかもしれん！」
シンジの頭の中に碇司令の声が響く。
ゲンドウ「EVAから離れたとき、お前は人でなくなる。」


ネルフ本局司令部のフロアをただならぬ表情で歩いてくるリツコ、そのまま管制室の扉をくぐり抜ける。リツコ「EVA01、00との通信は、復帰しましたか！？」
冬月「いやまだだ。広侍域の電磁嵐はまだ続いているよ。回復の見込みはない」
伊吹「朗報もあります。先ほど青葉さんが、まだ生きている民間の衛星を見つけて軌道変更成功しました！1EVAとレーザー回線で通話できそうです」
青葉「とはいうものの、使える軌道に乗せるまでに丸1日はかかりそうです」
冬月「それより……先はど君の名前で出されたEVA01、00に対する作戦中止命令の理由を聞こう」 一同リツコを注視する。
コンソールへ進んで、内分のラボのコンピュータとつなぐリツコ。
リツコ「EVAを生み出すプロセスを……何度かのシミュレーションで、フオウチュンの誕生を確認しました。我々はEVAに対する価値基準を根本的に変える必要に迫られそうです」
ディスプレーに映し出される、分裂を続けるEVAの疑似細胞核。ところが途中から勾厳に遺伝情報が書き変わっていく。やがてそれが細胞そのものの変化に表われた瞬間、レーザーで光圧処理される。
ゲンドウ「続けたまえ」
リツコ「3体のか水準に達した。EVAがこの世に存在すると、その次には必ずフオウチュンが生まれる。これがEVAという種の絶対法則のようです」
日向「でもEVAは、すでに何体も存在していたじゃないですか。なぜいまになって？」
ゲンドウ「EVAはそれ自体が進化する」
冬月「その中でもお眼鏡にかなったEVAはわずかというわけか。しかし、フオウチュンはともかくEVAを造ったのは人間だ。種としての行動といえるかな」
リツコ「EVAはなぜ人型なのやでしょう。文明をもつ生物が、同じ形のものを見つけたら……」
冬月「興味をもつな」
リツコ「さらに科学技術が進歩すれば」
冬月「まず、調べるために複製してみて……！？ばかな！……初めから、原始の時代から仕組まれていたことだというのか！？」
ゲンドウ「タネはひとつだった。我々がそのタネを増やし、育てた」
リツコ「そして、収穫のときは来たのです」


フオウチュンのテリトリーに侵入しつつあるEVA01。
不思議ときっぱりとした顔のシンジ。
シンジ「あ」
地面に落ちている〝死神の背骨〟を拾い上げるEV


Page 83

A01。
シンジ「アスカの落とし物か……」
人狼からEVAの指し示すその向こうへ……いまシンジは、ヒ卜という存在がその聞を初抽出うものだと感じ始めている。
月下の瓦礁の衡を進むEVA01。右肩に〝死神の背骨〟を装備している。やや、遠巻きにして、人狼たちが後を追う。
川を飛び越えるEVA01。うずくまるように着地するほどの大ジャンプ。着地の音響が消えた無音の街に、衝撃で述統的にふれあう〝死神の背骨〟の、鱗状アーマーの金属的な残響が尾を引〈。
やがて、まわりにいた人狼の姿が消えていることに気付くシンジ。踏み越えてはならないエリアに侵入したらしい（実は1匹だけ、EVA01の後を追い続けている人狼がいる。聞に光る目は人狼のそれだが、その姿はアスカだ。廃櫨から廃他地へ飛び移り、EVA01を見下ろしている)。
フオウチュンのオブジェが一望できる丘の上に立つEVA01。
シンジ「さて……と」


すでに神の領域ごときは侵して久しく
少年の背の羽は黒く変わって抜け落ちた
すべてに近い人々が人狼となったいま
ヒトという存在は
限りなく自分ひとりの意味に近づき
全体の中の1個とする自分自身の認識を
彼は脱ぎ捨てつつある
彼はことごとくを打ち払ってでも前へ進む道を選ぶ


(以上、プロローグ終わり)

chunks.content:
Translations for the contents of Yamashita & Kio's book Sore o Nasumono.

Neon Genesis Evangelion TV Last Scenario Prot
This is the original text and the English translation of the unused TV Series ending scenario, translation by RockManBalls. Corrections by Xard. This transcript is currently incomplete.

English Translation
It might be surprising given what ultimately resulted, but during the initial development of the Anime series, Supervisor Anno took on something of a passive role, and staff would regularly bounce ideas off of him in order to flesh out the story. As time passed, and I was put in charge, I received an early scenario manuscript by fax. When I went to return the fax, I jotted down some left over ideas just off the top of my head. I still get a good laugh out of it to this day.

Original Japanese
作品の内容からは意外な印象かもしれませんが、庵野監督はアニメ制作の初期段階においては受け身の姿勢をとり、客スタッフにアイデアを出させぶつけ合うことで、話の骨格に肉付けをします。てな経過から、設定担当である私のところにも脚本の初期稿がファックスでやってきます。それについて思いついたことは電話で返すのですが、ファックスで返却するとこのように内容が残り、あとから見かえすとなかなか笑えます。

Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film
This is the original text and the English translation of the unused Movie scenario, translation by RockManBalls. This transcript is currently incomplete.

English Translation
Page74
Shin Seiki Evangelion Original Film Novel
"The Long-Awaited People."
[Translator's Notes: "Gekijouban shousetsu," meaning the book based on the movie that was never made.]

During the development of the film, Yamashita, being asked to submit ideas, thought of an original story he'd conceived mid-way through the television show. Created in collaboration with Kio [omitted]*, the utterly ridiculous work was

made.]

During the development of the film, Yamashita, being asked to submit ideas, thought of an original story he'd conceived mid-way through the television show. Created in collaboration with Kio [omitted]*, the utterly ridiculous work was eventually presented to Supervisor Anno. Rather than a sequel to Evangelion TV series, contents of the script are better described as All Star Show featuring the whole cast.
[*Translator's Notes: As I know it, "氏宅" is a placeholder for someone who doesn't want to give their name, usually their surname. Like "Takashi S氏宅." ]

The last Angel had been defeated. Having just confronted the Angel threat, humanity began to wield EVA against EVA. As this abuse of EVA technology continued, an enormous conflict erupted. This is the era in which our story begins. 

Page 75
During this conflict, NERV General Headquarters' Special Forces Division, along with EVA 01, attacked NERV's Euro-Germany Branch. Having confirmed the existence of "that," EVA 01 expanded its AT-Field, and NERV completely destroyed the facility with a neutron bomb. The "that" they were looking for was the Sinking-Type Equipment Universal Invasion Gun (Field Sinker), code-named the "Spine of the Death God." This new type of corrosive AT-Field worked by ways of a guaranteed-to-hit heavy particle bullet, which, when used against enemies with current AT-Field capacity, would almost always find its target with terrible speed. At the same time as construction of this secret weapon was underway, the Euro-Germany Branch used their political will to secure the return of their creation, EVA 02. In a single move, NERV Germany hid EVA 02 and the "Spine of the Death God" in accordance with their master plan. Before their troublesome dealings could be discovered by the international community, they were ultimately stopped by the NERV organization proper. Although NERV's Euro-Germany Branch has since been dismantled, as well as their brilliant achievement, EVA 02, this incident would serve as a catalyst, forever linking EVA technology to global military strategy. However, this turmoil would serve only as a small part of a greater disaster.  

How will these mysteries come to light?
Will they gather the technological strength they need?
Now, all is but a mystery...
However, those humanoid mechs have been built.
Humanity's creations.
But

a small part of a greater disaster.  

How will these mysteries come to light?
Will they gather the technological strength they need?
Now, all is but a mystery...
However, those humanoid mechs have been built.
Humanity's creations.
But those creations are EVA.

EVA has proven to be distinctly different, continually defying current technology. From the very beginnings of civilization, a powerful mental contamination was underway. Humanity had always been set in a certain direction...... 

Page 76
[Translator's Notes: Handwritten text in bottom-right reads: Thick beams of light shoot out from here. The light should be fine and particled, gradually getting brighter in target area. Despite radiating light in all directions, it should look like it's firing inwards.]

A contamination not unlike brainwashing gradually drove into each and every person. At first it infected the staff involved in the creation of the humanoid mechs. Soon followed the organization, the city, and finally the entire country. All began to move with a single purpose: they were commanded to build an EVA in their own image.

Fortune.

Does this name refer to it's nature, or that of it's existence? It is unknown what nation constructed Fortune. For the nation responsible was itself burnt to the ground... And it was from that day on that a worldwide mental contamination began.

Beasts in the shape of men run throughout a ruined city. They do not speak. They do not use tools. They run on all fours. They communicate only with powerful gazes in order to protect their territory and the prosperity of their brood. They are wolf-men. When this mental contamination first infected normal humans, they lived what could be described as a life of culture. But eventually this mental contamination became an organic one, and in a short period of time, their bodies transformed into something like the werewolves of fairy-tales. And this abnormality is not confined to a single city. Now, throughout the world, this destructive form is common among human beings. Boasting this world-wide influence, the composition of humanity has changed drastically. Human beings, as intellectual life-forms, is left with only a whisper of the remaining populous, with the amount of destruction only accelerating. Those still remaining live in constant fear of this previously unseen, and unusual, contamination. Within a ruined city, standing erect atop the rubble of the city's countless buildings, the wolf-men look to the sky. Together they face the moon and howl.

The view from the monitor displayed a

this previously unseen, and unusual, contamination. Within a ruined city, standing erect atop the rubble of the city's countless buildings, the wolf-men look to the sky. Together they face the moon and howl.

The view from the monitor displayed a structure in the shape of a pyramid. Flames surround the outlying ruins of this perfect geometric structure, glistening horribly as it suppresses the invading wolf-men. This pyramid is NERV's Central Experimental City. It is no exaggeration to say that it has truly become humanity's final fortress. In this city, they wield meager Jamming Technology to bear the onslaught. Namely, they've been able to disrupt the mental contamination waves, while maintaining the facility's structural integrity, through use of Interference Wave Oscillation. Because of this, those within the walls are protected from the mental contamination waves pouring down on nearly the rest of the entire world. The technology itself was originally used in EVA's experimental phase of construction, used to protect against mental disaster (Psychological Hazard). In a word, the use of this technology has spared these lucky people from total mental contamination. In the center of the facility, in a bored out hole mirroring the shape of a mortar, EVA 01 stands ready. An Electrical Phase Contact Unit resembling feathers or a tail has been installed onto EVA 01's back, allowing her to operate on her own volition, without needing a pilot. This Reflector Hole, as it's now called, was once used as an orbital observation system receiver, but has since been converted into an EVA "private room." As if listening out for something, EVA 01 watches the starry sky above.

In a roof-top corridor outside the facility, Hyuga and Aoba looked to EVA curiously. "She looks like some kind of a poet or philosopher, doesn't she?" Aoba said.

"She certainly looks like she's thinking about something," agreed Hyuga. "When EVA goes off to battle, before the pilot unleashes her rage, EVA 01 seems so calm and intellectual... Like she's trying to compose an understanding of something she can't completely grasp, but still knowing more than just a small notion. Who knows what she's thinking."

Page 77
"I don't know either..." Aoba said, lowering his gaze as he looked outside the facility. Beyond the line of fire, in the center of a rubble-covered road, Aoba caught a glimpse of the distant shadows of the wolf-men. "But

don't know either..." Aoba said, lowering his gaze as he looked outside the facility. Beyond the line of fire, in the center of a rubble-covered road, Aoba caught a glimpse of the distant shadows of the wolf-men. "But I think those guys outside think about even less. Still, don't they seem to be getting a little more... sophisticated? Or... something like that. I actually think it's somewhat incredible." Aoba turned to Hyuga. "I know that must sound weird, huh?"

"You're not alone. People are just afraid to say it," Hyuga said, looking, too, at the wolf-men gathered on the road. "They suddenly attack the very thing we're supposed to protect. In truth, there are only a few people in this entire facility. This equipment prevents us from going outside, not to mention we only have our emergency stores to live on... Still, we've managed to keep on fighting one way or another, even though we're up against an enemy we still don't fully understand." He took a deep breath. "The people of this city try to act normal, but they're troubled. They can't help but depend on wishful thinking. They're looking through EVA's eyes to the future." Unknown if she was truly thinking or not, EVA continued to stand alone in deep meditation.

Every engineer in the facility had piled into the Plug Regulation Room, investigating something in 01's Entry Plug. Using a fiber scope--something resembling a gastrocamera--they looked over an exposed circuit board. Two engineers in the plug's interior enlarged the camera's view with a computer display.

"Hold on!" Engineer A yelled. "Go back a little! Hey, wait, what is that...?"

"There's no doubt about it!" Engineer B said. He turned to address the others. "Quickly! Bring Dr. Akagi over here!"

They saw through the Neuro-Computer Zoom Matrix that a single large synapse had wrapped itself around a nerve cord.

"There must be a leak on the side of the Basic Order Line's cut-feedback," Ritsuko announced. "Is it true that EVA did this itself? What is the possibility that it was re-written by a third party?"

"There's no evidence of anti-body program or structural defense failure," Engineer A said. "Of course, in theory, it is possible to trick the anti-body program all together. Still, irregular fluctuations have been recorded. We can only assume that EVA's own consciousness

of anti-body program or structural defense failure," Engineer A said. "Of course, in theory, it is possible to trick the anti-body program all together. Still, irregular fluctuations have been recorded. We can only assume that EVA's own consciousness might have re-written the information. The irregularity was recorded roughly one month ago... That would put it during EVA 01's Deep Stage accident..."

Ritsuko, without using the fiber scope, had crawled into the narrow cylinder and over the seat of the plug. Pointing a small lighted loupe, she quickly located the problem. She lightly furrowed her brow just as the engineer finished speaking.

He continued: "I can't explain why, but that incident seems to be the cause of this."

"There seems to be no question about it..." Ritsuko said. Climbing her way out of the plug, Ritsuko lost her grip and began to slide down. Without a moments delay, an engineer grabbed her hand and pulled her back up. "Thank you..." she said. "We need to know what kinds of effects this circuit-change will have on the pilot during operation." She dropped down to the main floor and looked over her team of engineers. "Are there any suggestions?"

They all wore grave expressions. Engineer C said flatly, "There shouldn't be any effect."

In a previous battle

A nude image of shinji yelled out boldly, letting loose a tremendous punch. Moving in concert, EVA doubled his actions. Shinji had become the EVA itself. This is what is called the Deep Stage. The punch struck a tank-like robot, but its shield and EVA's AT-Field collided, nulling any contact.

"01 to Control!" Shinji shouted. "This guy's harder than I thought!" Shinji's voice echoed inside the Entry Plug, but he hadn't spoken aloud. A visor over his eyes flickered strange patterns of light, while his body radiated a soft, glowing light as he laid back in the plug's seat as if in a deep sleep.

"I'm authorizing use of the Monoatomic Ribbon," Misato ordered.

"What about back-up from 00?!" Shinji said. "Is Ayanami in battle position yet?!"

Engineer C's voice was getting smaller and smaller as he spoke. "Normal synchronization or not, using Super Synchronize during Deep Stage will cause the pilot's consciousness to be completely transferred to EVA," he said. "The pilot will begin to think

er C's voice was getting smaller and smaller as he spoke. "Normal synchronization or not, using Super Synchronize during Deep Stage will cause the pilot's consciousness to be completely transferred to EVA," he said. "The pilot will begin to think that he, himself, is EVA."

"However," Engineer A said, "while his consciousness is absent, his body will cease to function. Until the pilot's mind returns, EVA will be in control of his body."

"In that scenario," Engineer B said, "EVA's brain is no longer being controlled by the host computer, nor is Shinji in control of his body. Put simply, EVA and the pilot will have exchanged both their minds and bodies. Operating with the Basic Order Line cut, and without receiving commands from the body control program, we believe EVA's consciousness will flood rapidly into the pilot's body."

"No, that's not possible," Engineer D said. "Recordings from prior experiments have shown that the pilot will most certainly die from ego collapse. The structure of EVA's mind is completely different from a human's. If EVA's mind enters Shinji and he is unable to control it, he will cease to be!"

Asuka stood on the gate outside the facility. Those inside the fortress didn't know she was there. フオウチュンのオブジェが一望できる丘の上に立つEVA01。
